nativeRange.habitat: Riversides and periodically flooded marshy areas in the Amazon basin and other South American wetlands

summary: Alternanthera reineckii, commonly sold as Scarlet Temple, is a South American stem plant valued for its red to purple foliage. Intense coloration requires bright light, CO2 injection, and iron-rich fertilization, placing it in the intermediate range of planted-tank care.
careNotes: Alternanthera reineckii is one of the few Alternanthera species that grows reliably submersed, and is the classic 'red temple' widely sold for planted aquariums. It is broadly suited to a temperature range of 22–28 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, and soft-to-moderately-hard water (gH 2–12, kH 2–10). Lighting is the single most important variable for deep red coloration: medium light keeps the plant alive, but bright light (generally above 40 PAR) combined with CO2 injection and iron-rich liquid or root fertilization is generally needed for the rich red-to-purple leaf colour the species is known for. Without CO2 and strong light, leaves tend to fade toward orange or green, and lower leaves can drop. The plant prefers a nutrient-rich substrate — aquasoil or a standard substrate topped up with root tabs — and benefits from both water-column dosing and root feeding. Sudden changes in parameters should be avoided, as they can trigger leaf melt and stem rot, particularly in freshly planted tissue-culture specimens transitioning from emersed (above-water) to submersed growth. Several named cultivars are commonly available in the trade: 'Mini', 'Rosanervig' (distinguished by pink-veined leaves), 'Scarlet', 'Mini Sunset', 'Cardinalis', and 'Pink'. Most share the same husbandry as the species-level form, though 'Mini' is a true foreground or carpet variant that stays considerably smaller than the standard species.
breedingNotes: Propagated by nipping off the terminal bud of a mature stem and replanting the cutting in the substrate; this also encourages lateral side-shoot growth on the mother plant. Stem fragments can also be floated until they develop roots and then planted. When grown emersed, the plant produces small flowers but seed propagation is uncommon in the hobby.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Used for lighting (medium-high), CO2 (required), growth rate, height range (~10-30 cm), care level (easy-moderate), pH (6.5-7.5), native range, family. Primary product page rather than the Tropica search URL because the Tropica search root returned no usable per-species detail; Tropica's per-cultivar pages were used as supplementary.

additional.name: Tropica
additional.notes: Mini cultivar page — used for cross-checking lighting (medium), CO2 (medium, 6-14 mg/L), growth rate (medium), care difficulty (medium). The Mini variant is a smaller foreground form; Tropica's 'Pink' cultivar (https://tropica.com/en/plants/plantdetails/4436/4436) was also consulted and reports height 20-30+ cm and South American origin.

additional.name: Freshwater Aquarium Plants
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (22-28 °C optimal), lighting (moderate-high), CO2 (required), height (25-50 cm), placement (mid-background), propagation (terminal-bud cuttings), and native range (Amazon South America).
